seoTitle: Point Wilson vs Pomborneit — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Point Wilson and Pomborneit. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Point Wilson vs Pomborneit: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Point Wilson and Pomborneit.
heading: Point Wilson vs Pomborneit — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Point Wilson, Victoria and Pomborneit, Victoria. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Pomborneit has a lower overall climate risk score (36/100) compared to Point Wilson (42/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Point Wilson or Pomborneit safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Point Wilson has a flood risk score of 50/100, while Pomborneit scores 57/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Point Wilson scores 65/100 for bushfire risk. Pomborneit scores 21/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Point Wilson or Pomborneit?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Pomborneit has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.73% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
